mechanism_narrative: A sustained high level of industrial production (INDPRO >100) signals robust manufacturing activity and corporate profitability, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The Rank IC of INDPRO level vs 20-day forward returns falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in the unemployment rate (more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months) indicates a tightening labor market that boosts consumer confidence and spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are negative in at least 60% of trigger events, or the mean return is not significantly above zero (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation is rising (month-over-month increase) while the federal funds rate remains below 1%, real interest rates are negative, encouraging equity investment and producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with rising inflation
failure_condition: The interaction term (CPIAUCSL change * low FEDFUNDS) has a Rank IC below 0.03 or its coefficient in a linear regression is not significantly positive (p > 0.05) over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During a tightening monetary cycle (FEDFUNDS > 1%), a decline in housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening residential investment and broader economic slowdown, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods when FEDFUNDS > 1% and HOUST falls month-over-month, the 60-day forward return is positive more than 55% of the time, or the mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising real retail sales (RSAFS) reflect strong consumer demand and corporate earnings growth, consistently driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC of RSAFS level vs 5-day forward returns is below 0.03 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) coupled with high retail sales (RSAFS) signals a self-reinforcing consumption-employment cycle that boosts equity prices over the following month.
failure_condition: The joint signal (PAYEMS change > 0 and RSAFS change > 0) produces a mean 20-day forward return less than zero or not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over the entire sample.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is in a rising trend (FEDFUNDS increases by 25bp over 3 months), the effect of strong GDP growth (GDPC1) on forward returns becomes negative because it reinforces tightening expectations.
failure_condition: In rising-rate regimes, the correlation between GDPC1 growth and 60-day forward returns is positive or zero, contradicting the hypothesized negative relationship.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) exceeds 3%, it triggers fears of Fed tightening and negative real income effects, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When CPI YoY exceeds 3%, the 5-day forward return is positive more than 50% of the time, or the average return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).
